reasoning:
Changes to the Dow Jones Industrial Average are historically infrequent, occurring roughly once every 2-4 years, unless forced by major corporate actions like spinoffs or mergers. The DJIA recently underwent component shifts in February 2024 (Amazon replacing Walgreens) and November 2024 (Nvidia and Sherwin-Williams replacing Intel and Dow Inc.). Therefore, an additional spontaneous change within roughly 7–8 months of November 2024 is highly unlikely. 

There are currently roughly 66 days left until the July 1, 2025 resolution date. A base rate estimate for this two-month window is less than 5%, and conditioned on the recent 2024 updates, the real odds are even lower. Furthermore, targeted web searches for upcoming spinoffs, mergers, or any analyst rumors concerning current Dow 30 components (including troubled stocks like Boeing) yield zero indications of any imminent index review by S&P Dow Jones Indices. Because removals are rarely telegraphed ahead of time but often triggered by known corporate actions which are missing right now, I am putting the probability at 3% to reflect base low-level tail risk of an unannounced, discretionary decision.
